text:
ByKasey Nelson,

Former Contributor.

Team USA’s Suni Lee successfully defended her Olympic bronze medalist title on uneven bars on Sunday inside of Bercy Arena in Paris. 17-year-old Kaylia Nemour of Algeria took the gold to become Africa’s first Olympic medalist in gymnastics. And China’s Qiu Qiyuan, who is also 17 years old, grabbed silver.

For uneven bars event finals, each gymnast performs a routine, and the difficulty and execution scores are combined for the overall score. The higher the difficulty, the better your chances are to medal. The top eight gymnasts' bar scores from qualification compete for a chance at medaling. Simone Biles came in ninth place, so she was the first reserve (alternate).

The favorites going into today’s competition were Nemour, who led in qualifications, Qiyuan who was second in qualifications, and Lee, who came in third in qualifications and earned the bronze in the 2020 Tokyo Games.

First up is China’s 16-year-old Zhang Yihan. She  mounts the bar and shows beautiful pirouette work but misses the bar on her Hindorff (a clear hip Tkatchev) for one full point in deductions. While she gets back up and finishes the routine, her score is a disappointing 12.8 score, which is almost two points lower than her qualifying score.

Italy’s Alice D’Amato shows stunning lines and dismounts with a double front, scoring a  14.733 and taking an early lead.

Belgium’s Nina Derwael, the reigning Olympic bars champion, is up next coming back to the biggest stage after left shoulder surgery last year. She hits her 6.4 difficulty-valued routine, but takes a hop and step back on her full-twisting double back dismount to earn a 14.766, which is enough to take the lead over D’Amato.

Becky Downie of Great Britain falls on her Stalder Tkatchev but finishes strong with a stuck full-twisting double back. She scores a 13.633 due to the one-point deduction.

Qiyuan, who has the highest level of difficulty on bars with at a 7.2, shows stunning pirouette work. She also sticks her full-twisting double back to fire a 15.5, surging into first and scoring more than four-tenths higher than her qualifying score.

Algeria’s Kaylia Nemour, who matches Qiyuan’s difficulty level of a 7.2, performs a big laid out Tkatchev straight to Pac to Shaposhnikova. She nearly sticks her toe full to double layout dismount and is emotional after completing the stunning routine. Nemour bests Qiyuan with a 15.7, and no one is catching that score.

Next up is Germany’s Helen Kevric. She hits her routine and dismounts with a full- twisting double back and a hop forward to score a 14.566. She sits in fifth.

And the final competitor on uneven bars is Lee. She mounts the bar with a toe on to laid-out Tkatchev straight into a Bhardwaj (full-twisting pac salto) and dismounts with a full-twisting double back and a step back. Will she repeat for a bronze? Lee earns a 14.8 to clinch the bronze medal for the second time in her career. She now owns six Olympic medals: two gold, one silver and three bronze. Nemour takes the gold and Qiyuan grabs the silver medal.
title: Women’s Gymnastics 2024 Olympic Bars Final Results
author: Kasey Nelson
description: Team USA’s Suni Lee successfully defended her Olympic bronze medalist title on uneven bars on Sunday inside of Bercy Arena in Paris.
keywords: Suni Lee Olympics,Suni Lee Wins Bronze on Bars,Kaylia Nemour Wins Gold On Bars,Qiu Qiyuan Wins Silvers on Bars,Women's Gymnastics 2024 Olympics Results,Women's Gymnastics Olympic Bars Final Results,Women's Gymnastics Paris 2024 Olympics,Suni Lee Paris Games,Simone Biles Olympics,Jordan Chiles Olympics
